reasoning_content:
The user wants me to extract the layout of the provided document image into a JSON format containing bounding boxes.

1.  **Analyze the image:** The image is a technical drawing/datasheet for an electronic component package (SOT-23). It contains:
    *   Header: Logo, Title, Package info, Date.
    *   Main Diagrams: Top view, Side view, End view, Detail A, Recommended Mounting Footprint.
    *   Table: Dimensions in Millimeters (MIN, NOM, MAX).
    *   Notes: Text block explaining notes.
    *   Marking Diagram: Generic marking explanation.
    *   Footer: Document number, description, page number, legal text, copyright.

2.  **Strategy:** I will go through the document section by section, identifying text blocks and graphical elements, and estimating their bounding boxes [ymin, xmin, ymax, xmax].

    *   **Header:**
        *   Logo: Top left.
        *   Title: Top right.
        *   Package Info: Center top.
        *   Date: Top right, below title.
        *   Scale: Below the 3D model.

    *   **Diagrams (Left/Center):**
        *   Top View: Includes labels A, B, D, E, H_E, 1, 2, 3, e, 3X b.
        *   Detail "A": Zoomed in view of the lead.
        *   Side View: Shows height A and A1.
        *   End View: Shows angle T and lead shape.
        *   Generic Marking Diagram: Box with XXXM.
        *   Recommended Mounting Footprint: Bottom center diagram.

    *   **Table (Right):**
        *   Header: MILLIMETERS.
        *   Columns: DIM, MIN, NOM, MAX.
        *   Rows: A, A1, b, c, D, E, e, L, L1, HE, T.

    *   **Notes (Right, below table):**
        *   Numbered list 1-4.

    *   **Footer:**
        *   Document Number box.
        *   Description box.
        *   Page number box.
        *   Legal text block.
        *   Copyright line.

3.  **Refining Bounding Boxes (Mental Draft & Estimation):**
    *   *Logo*: [33, 95, 66, 299]
    *   *Title*: [572, 34, 61, 898] -> "MECHANICAL CASE OUTLINE PACKAGE DIMENSIONS"
    *   *Package Info*: [102, 360, 145, 634] -> "SOT-23..."
    *   *Date*: [142, 765, 154, 893] -> "DATE 14 AUG 2024"
    *   *Scale*: [159, 108, 167, 163] -> "SCALE 4:1"
    *   *Table*: [188, 612, 456, 880] (approximate area covering the whole table structure)
    *   *Notes*: [464, 649, 592, 876]
    *   *Marking Diagram*: [521, 183, 609, 263] (The box itself)
    *   *Footprint*: [510, 433, 646, 638] (The diagram area)
    *   *Footer Info*: [845, 100, 890, 898] (The main footer table)

4.  **Detailed Extraction:** I will now iterate through specific text elements and regions to create the JSON list.

    *   **Top Section:**
        *   Logo: [33, 95, 66, 299]
        *   Title: [34, 583, 65, 898]
        *   Subtitle: [51, 710, 65, 898]
        *   Package Name: [102, 360, 145, 634]
        *   Date: [142, 765, 154, 893]
        *   Scale: [159, 108, 167, 163]

    *   **Diagrams & Labels:**
        *   Top View labels (A, B, D, E, etc.): Scattered. I'll group the main view area.
        *   Detail A: [367, 478, 380, 553]
        *   Side View: [408, 187, 420, 252]
        *   End View: [449, 416, 461, 483]
        *   Generic Marking Diagram Title: [521, 196, 533, 299]
        *   Marking Diagram Box: [563, 183, 609, 263]
        *   Marking Legend: [617, 143, 638, 313]
        *   Footprint Title: [657, 463, 683, 592]
        *   Footprint Diagram: [510, 433, 646, 638]

    *   **Table:**
        *   Header: [188, 612, 210, 880]
        *   Row headers (A, A1, b...): [218, 649, 450, 675]
        *   Data cells: [218, 706, 450, 853]

    *   **Notes:**
        *   Notes Title: [464, 649, 472, 683]
        *   Note Text: [472, 649, 592, 876]

    *   **Footer:**
        *   Document Number Label: [845, 108, 857, 246]
        *   Document Number Value: [845, 273, 857, 383]
        *   Description Label: [870, 137, 882, 246]
        *   Description Value: [870, 273, 882, 563]
        *   Page Number: [870, 796, 882, 882]
        *   Legal Text: [904, 108, 940, 888]
        *   Copyright: [963, 108, 973, 335]
        *   Website: [963, 796, 973, 895]

5.  **Final Polish:** Ensure coordinates are reasonable and cover the visual elements accurately. The JSON structure needs to be clean. I will combine adjacent text where it forms a single logical block (like the title).

    *   *Self-Correction on Table:* The table is dense. I'll represent the main header and the body.
    *   *Self-Correction on Diagrams:* I'll focus on the main labels and titles associated with the diagrams rather than every single dimension line, as that would be too granular and clutter the JSON. I will capture the "Top View", "Side View", "End View", "Detail A", "Generic Marking Diagram", and "Recommended Mounting Footprint" labels and their associated diagrams.

    Let's refine the coordinates for the final output.
    - Logo: [33, 95, 66, 299]
    - Title: [34, 583, 65, 898]
    - Package Info: [102, 102, 145, 634] (Wait, y-coords are 102-145, x-coords 360-634) -> [102, 360, 145, 634]
    - Date: [142, 765, 154, 893]
    - Scale: [159, 108, 167, 163]
    - Table Header: [188, 612, 210, 880]
    - Table Body: [218, 649, 450, 853]
    - Notes: [464, 649, 592, 876]
    - Marking Diagram: [521, 196, 609, 263] (Title + Box)
    - Footprint: [657, 463, 683, 592] (Title) + [510, 433, 646, 638] (Diagram)
    - Footer: [845, 100, 890, 898]

    Okay, ready to generate the JSON.
